privileged_info_summary: This task uses real, publicly-available chemical data from the NOAA CAMEO Chemicals database. The two chemicals involved are Sulfuric Acid (UN 1830, CAS 7664-93-9) and Sodium Cyanide (UN 1689, CAS 143-33-9). The expected reaction is confirmed by chemistry: when sulfuric acid reacts with sodium cyanide, it produces hydrogen cyanide (HCN) gas according to the equation H₂SO₄ + 2NaCN → 2HCN + Na₂SO₄. Hydrogen cyanide is extremely toxic (IDLH 50 ppm) and represents the primary hazard in this scenario. The task requires using the legitimate CAMEO Chemicals web tool at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/ to document predicted hazards. The scenario is based on a realistic industrial incident involving forklift damage near chemical storage, and output must be saved to ~/Documents/reactivity_report.txt.
